what_it_does: A static preview card showing the gold-circle icon family as a single reference image (icon-library-gold-circles.jpg), with copy explaining its use (entity badges that 'count' as system components) and a metadata row specifying ring stroke (1.5–2px gold), inner glyph colour, badge sizes (32/48/64), and the asset path.
observations: No @dsCard annotation comment (like its bronze sibling, unlike the live AI/nav cards).
Displays a JPG sheet (../assets/icons/icon-library-gold-circles.jpg) rather than live SVG.
Describes each glyph as sitting inside a thin gold ring at a uniform size, used for entity badges in architecture diagrams and marketing collateral.
Notes the top desaturation row is 'the canonical state-strength gradient'.
Metadata row hardcodes spec values: Ring 1.5–2px gold, Inner matching gold glyph, Sizes 32/48/64.
Card chrome uses tokens (var(--bg-surface), var(--r-md), var(--font-body)).
standouts: Introduces the semantic notion that a gold-circle icon 'counts' as a system component (entity badge), distinguishing it from a plain glyph.
Names a 'canonical state-strength gradient' (the desaturation row) — a documented visual scale.
conventions: Uses _card.css and design tokens for card chrome.
Omits the @dsCard annotation used by the live preview cards.
States icon spec values as literal copy rather than token references (documentation card, not a styled consumer).
concerns: Spec values (ring width, sizes) are literal copy; if the canonical icon spec changes this card must be updated by hand to stay true.
notes: A static image-sheet card documenting the gold-circle entity-badge icon family, its ring/size spec, and the canonical state-strength desaturation gradient.
questions: What exactly does the 'state-strength gradient' (desaturation row) encode, and where is it consumed?
Is the gold here the same value as the --accent-gold token used by the live nav active state?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it documents the gold-circle icon family and its spec via an image and a metadata row, as stated.
apparent_themes: Entity badges as system components ('icons that count')
intention_signals: Trying to define the gold-circle badge family as the canonical way to depict an icon that represents a system component, with a fixed ring/size spec and a state-strength gradient for showing component strength/state.
novelty: The 'icon that counts as a system component' concept plus a named canonical state-strength desaturation gradient for badges.
contribution: Contributes the gold-circle entity-badge icon family, its ring/size spec (1.5–2px gold ring, sizes 32/48/64), and the canonical state-strength desaturation gradient.
summary_for_embedding: A claude-ds preview card documenting the gold-circle entity-badge icon family via a reference image and spec row (1.5–2px gold ring, sizes 32/48/64) plus a canonical state-strength desaturation gradient.
intention_for_embedding: To define the gold-circle badge family as the canonical depiction of an icon that represents a system component, with a fixed spec and a state-strength gradient.
